title: Hypothesis Testing for a Binomial Proportion
description: Set up and carry out a hypothesis test for a population proportion using the binomial distribution, including finding critical regions, interpreting p-values, and understanding Type I and Type II errors.
objectives: State null and alternative hypotheses (H0, H1) for a population proportion, including one-tailed and two-tailed forms
Use the binomial distribution to find the critical region for a test at a given significance level
Decide whether an observed value lies in the critical region and draw a conclusion in the context of the question
Explain and calculate a p-value as the probability of a result at least as extreme as the one observed
Distinguish between a Type I error (rejecting a true H0) and a Type II error (failing to reject a false H0)

lessons.title: Finding a critical region from the binomial distribution
Drawing conclusions, p-values, and Type I/II errors
